strategy_criteria_summary: USA-listed companies reporting earnings next week, sorted by market cap. This deliberately broad Finviz screen preserves the top five visible rows; optionability, liquidity, trend, news, fundamentals, and contract quality are validated downstream.
scoring_reasons: trend alignment was clearly supportive|market/sector environment was clearly supportive|price structure was clearly supportive|best contract was long_call 2026-05-29 97
decision_reasoning: User is long_only, which disqualifies the GME long put outright. Among the remaining long-call candidates, IONQ shows by far the strongest directional profile: +80% 20d, +51% 50d, RS vs SPY +72%, RS vs sector +59%, in a supportive QQQ/tech tape. MP has comparable momentum and a Wedbush $90 PT, but every listed MP contract expires 2026-05-08 — one day after earnings with unknown timing — which is a binary gamma bet, and IV (~158% on the 70-strike) guarantees severe crush. CSCO is a cleaner structure (40% IV, 5/29 expiry) but needs a 7.5% move with weak volume (0.18x avg) and negative RS vs its sector. IONQ's 5/15 49.5 call gives the best balance: strongest trend, ATM strike, expiry far enough past earnings to survive IV reset and participate in continuation. Main risk is the ~101% IV — a muted print could cause the premium to bleed even on a flat-to-up move. Score lands in Standard recommend band.
